title: 1. Basic Usage
text: Six small synthetic rasters, built directly cell coordinates, cover useful range shape behaviour: disk (reference shape every index treats optimal), pinwheel (four spiky, rotationally symmetric blades), dumbbell (two round masses joined thin bar), ring (disk centre hollowed ), L-shape (simple right-angle notch), patchy raster (several disjoint patches grown independently, never touching - genuine multi-part shape, raster analogue sf MULTIPOLYGON). first five single, connected pieces; patchy , shows section .
code: make_disk <- function(n = 81, radius = 30) {   r <- rast(nrows = n, ncols = n, xmin = 0, xmax = n, ymin = 0, ymax = n, crs = "local")   cx <- init(r, "x") - n / 2; cy <- init(r, "y") - n / 2   ifel(sqrt(cx^2 + cy^2) <= radius, 1, 0) }  make_pinwheel <- function(n = 81, blades = 4, r_max = 35) {   r <- rast(nrows = n, ncols = n, xmin = 0, xmax = n, ymin = 0, ymax = n, crs = "local")   cx <- init(r, "x") - n / 2; cy <- init(r, "y") - n / 2   theta <- (atan2(cy, cx) + 2 * pi) %% (2 * pi)   sector_width <- 2 * pi / blades   theta_local <- theta %% sector_width   # each blade sweeps from a point (theta_local = 0) out to r_max   # (theta_local = sector_width) - `blades` copies, evenly rotated   ifel(sqrt(cx^2 + cy^2) <= r_max * (theta_local / sector_width), 1, 0) }  make_dumbbell <- function(n = 81, r_ball = 16, bar_half_width = 4, gap = 50) {   r <- rast(nrows = n, ncols = n, xmin = 0, xmax = n, ymin = 0, ymax = n, crs = "local")   cx <- init(r, "x") - n / 2; cy <- init(r, "y") - n / 2   ball1 <- sqrt((cx + gap / 2)^2 + cy^2) <= r_ball   ball2 <- sqrt((cx - gap / 2)^2 + cy^2) <= r_ball   bar   <- (cx >= -gap / 2) & (cx <= gap / 2) & (abs(cy) <= bar_half_width)   ifel(ball1 | ball2 | bar, 1, 0) }  make_ring <- function(n = 81, r_outer = 32, r_inner = 18) {   r <- rast(nrows = n, ncols = n, xmin = 0, xmax = n, ymin = 0, ymax = n, crs = "local")   cx <- init(r, "x") - n / 2; cy <- init(r, "y") - n / 2   rad <- sqrt(cx^2 + cy^2)   ifel(rad <= r_outer & rad >= r_inner, 1, 0) }  make_L <- function(n = 81) {   r <- rast(nrows = n, ncols = n, xmin = 0, xmax = n, ymin = 0, ymax = n, crs = "local")   values(r) <- 0   r[10:70, 10:35] <- 1   r[45:70, 10:70] <- 1   r }  make_patchy <- function(n = 81,                                  num_patches = 5,                                  fill = 0.15,                                  buffer = TRUE) {          stopifnot(         num_patches > 0,         num_patches < n * n,         fill > 0,         fill <= 1     )          target <- max(num_patches, floor(fill * n * n))          ## raster     mat <- matrix(0L, n, n)          ## random seeds     seeds <- sample.int(n * n, num_patches)     mat[seeds] <- seq_len(num_patches)          ## maximum possible frontier size     max_frontier <- 2L * target          frontier_cell <- integer(max_frontier)     frontier_id   <- integer(max_frontier)          frontier_cell[seq_len(num_patches)] <- seeds     frontier_id[seq_len(num_patches)]   <- seq_len(num_patches)          nf <- num_patches     occupied <- num_patches          ## 4-neighbour growth     dr <- c(-1L, 1L, 0L, 0L)     dc <- c(0L, 0L, -1L, 1L)          while (occupied < target && nf > 0L) {                  ## random frontier element         i <- sample.int(nf, 1L)                  cell <- frontier_cell[i]         pid  <- frontier_id[i]                  ## remove by swap         frontier_cell[i] <- frontier_cell[nf]         frontier_id[i]   <- frontier_id[nf]         nf <- nf - 1L                  r <- ((cell - 1L) %% n) + 1L         c <- ((cell - 1L) %/% n) + 1L                  ord <- sample.int(4)                  for (k in ord) {                          rr <- r + dr[k]             cc <- c + dc[k]                          if (rr < 1L || rr > n || cc < 1L || cc > n)                 next                          if (mat[rr, cc] != 0L)                 next                          if (buffer) {                                  rs <- max(1L, rr - 1L):min(n, rr + 1L)                 cs <- max(1L, cc - 1L):min(n, cc + 1L)                                  nbr <- mat[rs, cs]                                  if (any(nbr > 0L & nbr != pid))                     next             }                          ## occupy             mat[rr, cc] <- pid             occupied <- occupied + 1L                          ## current cell remains active             nf <- nf + 1L             frontier_cell[nf] <- cell             frontier_id[nf]   <- pid                          ## new cell becomes active             nf <- nf + 1L             frontier_cell[nf] <- (cc - 1L) * n + rr             frontier_id[nf]   <- pid                          break         }     }          mat[mat == 0L] <- NA_integer_     mat[mat>0] <- 1 # Remove this if you want to preserve the patch id.          rast(mat, crs="local") }  shapes <- list(disk = make_disk(), pinwheel = make_pinwheel(), dumbbell = make_dumbbell(),                ring = make_ring(), L = make_L(), patchy = make_patchy())  shapes_stack <- rast(shapes) names(shapes_stack) <- names(shapes) plot(shapes_stack, col = c("white", "steelblue"), legend = FALSE, axes = FALSE, nc = 3)

code: p <- terra::patches(shapes$patchy, directions = 8, zeroAsNA = TRUE) terra::global(p, "max", na.rm = TRUE)[[1]]  # number of disjoint pieces [1] 7 gm_geodesic_span_index(shapes$patchy, n_points = 60, seed = 1)$index Warning in .warn_disconnected("gm_geodesic_span_index"): gm_geodesic_span_index(): the shape has more than one connected part (different, disconnected pieces) - mean geodesic distance is not defined across disconnected parts, so the index is not defined for this shape. Unlike gm_span_index()'s own Euclidean distance (always finite regardless of what lies between two points), this has no simple fix - a larger `n_points` makes an unreachable pair MORE likely to be sampled, not less, for a genuinely multi-part shape. what: weighted: using the raster’s own values as mass
title: 1. Basic Usage
text: Every index function takes weighted (default TRUE). shape always derived way - cell counts “inside” iff value neither NA exactly 0 - weighted controls whether values also act density/mass field throughout computation, ignored beyond defining shape. plain binary mask (every inside cell equal 1) gives identical answer either way, since ’s variation mass matter. genuinely continuous raster difference shows - , gradient increasing toward dumbbell’s right-hand ball:  plain ignored match exactly - weighted = FALSE continuous raster reproduces plain binary-mask index precisely, approximately, since substitutes constant mass every valid cell regardless raster’s values. weighted differs: mass concentrated toward right ball, shape’s effective centroid shifts, changing moment inertia relative unweighted (geometric) case. One thing weighted change: six classic metrics (hull_ratio, polsby_popper, width_length_ratio, reock, detour, exchange) weighted form - ’re properties shape’s boundary, convex hull, bounding circle alone, unaffected mass distributed inside . 0-valued cells considered outside shape regardless. don’t even weighted argument passed using gm_shape_indices(), silently ignored.
code: gradient <- init(shapes$dumbbell, "x") + 41  # 0 (left) to ~120 (right) weight_rast <- ifel(shapes$dumbbell == 1, gradient, NA) plot(weight_rast, col = hcl.colors(50, "viridis"), axes = FALSE,      main = "dumbbell, weighted by an x-gradient") plain    <- gm_moment_of_inertia_index(shapes$dumbbell)$index ignored  <- gm_moment_of_inertia_index(weight_rast, weighted = FALSE)$index weighted <- gm_moment_of_inertia_index(weight_rast, weighted = TRUE)$index data.frame(plain, ignored, weighted) plain   ignored  weighted 1 0.4108922 0.4108922 0.3708851

title: 1. Basic Usage

Sharing one argument name two conventions mean ordinary size = 3000 call - perfectly sensible three indices - silently also drive 3000 sequential whole-raster gridDist() calls gm_shape_indices()’s shared ..., geodesic pair reachable call (verified directly cause real, several-second slowdown two argument names split apart - see ?gm_geodesic_span_index). n_points needs noticeably larger size comparable precision: gridDist()’s angular quantization discrete grid adds noise top ordinary point-sampling noise, D D_ref carry Monte Carlo variability given sample size closed-form-reference indices - point costs whole-raster sweep rather coordinate lookup, n_points also , much lower, memory/time-derived ceiling (?gm_geodesic_span_index details).

title: 1. Basic Usage
text: gridmorph also exposes standard morphological operators directly terra rasters - erode(), dilate(), opening(), closing(), tophat(), bottomhat() - something terra doesn’t provide configurable structuring elements. kernel argument takes matrix (1 = included, NA = excluded), one three named shortcuts: opening() (erode dilate) removes small bright features narrower structuring element - exactly pinwheel’s thin, tapering blade tips:  Trimming thinnest blade tips barely moves convexity - pinwheel’s concavity comes gaps blades, tips , particular structuring element isn’t tool job. Morphological operators shape indices answer different questions: operators reshape raster; indices score whatever shape hand , opened . None six operators require 0/1 mask - generalize continuous (grayscale) categorical (label-image) rasters , genuinely different way, just relabelled version binary case. See vignette("b-morphological-operators") full tour, including tophat()/bottomhat() return real-valued residual continuous input boolean flag categorical input.

title: 1. Basic Usage
text: Every index package requires rast use planar (projected) coordinates - geographic (longitude/latitude) CRS rejected error. isn’t area, distance, perimeter can’t computed correctly geographic data general - terra’s expanse(), perim(), distance() correctly, via geodesic math, even geographic CRS. real problem narrower: package’s point-based geometry - line-crossing tests gm_convexity_index(), pairwise distances gm_span_index()/gm_radial_concentration_index(), moment-tensor calculations, minimum-enclosing-circle fitting - works directly rast’s x/y coordinate space Cartesian, isn’t yet geodesic-aware. six shapes used crs = "local", real (arbitrary) projected CRS terra provides exactly kind synthetic-grid use - real-world rasters need genuine projected CRS (state plane, UTM, similar) calling index . See ?gridmorph full policy, including happens CRS set .

title: 2. Morphological Operators
text: Every operator takes kernel argument: plain matrix 1 includes cell structuring element’s footprint NA excludes (matching terra::focal()’s w convention directly). Three named shortcuts build common shapes: hand-built matrix works , long non-NA entries exactly 1 - erode()/dilate() reject anything else clear error rather silently misbehaving (terra::focal() treats values multiplicative weights, boolean mask).

title: 2. Morphological Operators
text: operators’ original, still fully supported case: 1 = inside shape, 0/NA = outside. erode() shrinks shape one structuring-element radius; dilate() grows :  opening() (erode dilate) removes small bright features narrower structuring element without shrinking shape overall; closing() (dilate erode) fills small dark gaps without growing . compose directly - opening(mask, k) exactly dilate(erode(mask, k), k), independent implementation - makes natural denoising pair classification noise: random single-cell flips (spurious “” cells spurious “” holes) mostly disappear opening() closing():  tophat() (mask opening(mask)) highlights exactly small bright features opening() removed; bottomhat() (closing(mask) mask) highlights exactly small dark gaps closing() filled. return 0/1 raster, grid input.
code: r <- rast(nrows = 61, ncols = 61, xmin = 0, xmax = 61, ymin = 0, ymax = 61, crs = "local") cx <- init(r, "x") - 30.5 cy <- init(r, "y") - 30.5 disk <- ifel(sqrt(cx^2 + cy^2) <= 22, 1, 0)  par(mfrow = c(1, 3)) plot(erode(disk, se_disc(4)), col = c("white", "steelblue"), legend = FALSE, axes = FALSE, main = "erode") plot(disk, col = c("white", "steelblue"), legend = FALSE, axes = FALSE, main = "original") plot(dilate(disk, se_disc(4)), col = c("white", "steelblue"), legend = FALSE, axes = FALSE, main = "dilate") set.seed(1) noisy <- disk flipped <- sample(ncell(noisy), 80) values(noisy)[flipped] <- 1 - values(noisy)[flipped]  cleaned <- closing(opening(noisy, se_disc(1)), se_disc(1))  par(mfrow = c(1, 2)) plot(noisy, col = c("white", "steelblue"), legend = FALSE, axes = FALSE, main = "noisy (80 flipped cells)") plot(cleaned, col = c("white", "steelblue"), legend = FALSE, axes = FALSE, main = "opening then closing") cat("cells differing from the true disk - noisy:", sum(values(noisy) != values(disk)),     " cleaned:", sum(values(cleaned) != values(disk)), "\n") cells differing from the true disk - noisy: 80  cleaned: 7

code: disk_r <- rast(nrows = 51, ncols = 51, xmin = 0, xmax = 51, ymin = 0, ymax = 51, crs = "local") gcx <- init(disk_r, "x") - 25.5 gcy <- init(disk_r, "y") - 25.5 disk_mask <- ifel(sqrt(gcx^2 + gcy^2) <= 20, 1, 0) gradient <- ifel(disk_mask == 1, gcx + 30, NA) eroded <- erode(gradient, se_disc(4)) dilated <- dilate(gradient, se_disc(4)) shared_range <- range(c(values(eroded), values(gradient), values(dilated)), na.rm = TRUE)  par(mfrow = c(1, 3)) plot(eroded, col = hcl.colors(50, "viridis"), range = shared_range, axes = FALSE, main = "erode(gradient, se_disc(4))") plot(gradient, col = hcl.colors(50, "viridis"), range = shared_range, axes = FALSE, main = "gradient") plot(dilated, col = hcl.colors(50, "viridis"), range = shared_range, axes = FALSE, main = "dilate(gradient, se_disc(4))") plateau <- ifel(disk_mask == 1, 50, NA) bump <- ifel(sqrt((gcx - 10)^2 + (gcy + 5)^2) <= 2, 20, 0)  # radius 2, smaller than the se_disc(3) below weight_rast <- plateau + bump  weight_th <- tophat(weight_rast, se_disc(3)) par(mfrow = c(1, 2)) plot(weight_rast, col = hcl.colors(50, "viridis"), axes = FALSE, main = "weight_rast (plateau + one bump)") plot(weight_th, col = hcl.colors(50, "viridis"), axes = FALSE, main = "tophat(weight_rast, se_disc(3))") dip <- ifel(sqrt((gcx + 10)^2 + (gcy - 5)^2) <= 2, -20, 0) weight_rast2 <- plateau + dip  weight_bh <- bottomhat(weight_rast2, se_disc(3)) par(mfrow = c(1, 2)) plot(weight_rast2, col = hcl.colors(50, "viridis"), axes = FALSE, main = "weight_rast2 (plateau + one dip)") plot(weight_bh, col = hcl.colors(50, "viridis"), axes = FALSE, main = "bottomhat(weight_rast2, se_disc(3))")

text: Categorical rasters (terra::.factor() - discrete, unordered class codes, e.g. land-cover classification) genuinely different case, just relabelled version continuous one. Taking local min/max raw CODE NUMBERS mean something spatially codes happened carry real order, nominal categories don’t - ’s meaningful sense land-cover code 1 (say, forest) “less ” code 3 (say, urban). erode()/dilate() switch standard label-image morphology instead numeric min/max formulas : erode(): cell keeps label every cell neighbourhood shares exact label; otherwise becomes unlabelled (NA). Equivalent eroding class’s binary mask separately taking union - classes mutually exclusive, ’s never overlap resolve. dilate(): already-labelled cell left untouched; unlabelled (NA) cell filled majority label among labelled neighbours. Restricting fill NA cells keeps monotonic (defining property dilation - ever grows background, never reassigns existing foreground). blind majority filter applied everywhere instead smoothing operation, relabelling already-correct interior cells near strong neighbouring majority. One landcover raster stray class-2 cell poking class-1 territory one-cell interior gap - far enough class boundary ’s genuine gap, just eroded edge - shows four operators . circles mark two cells interest, spatial position every panel:  opening() (erode dilate) removes stray cell entirely - thin survive erosion, re-grown dilation since isn’t adjacent rest class 2 - tophat() (!.na(mask) & .na(opening(mask)), label-image analogue mask opening(mask)) flags exactly one cell, nothing else. gap works way direction: closing() (dilate erode) fills back class-1 neighbours, bottomhat() (.na(mask) & !.na(closing(mask))) flags exactly cell. Subtracting category codes way continuous residual meaningless either direction (“class 1 minus class 2” even mean?) - tophat()/bottomhat() return boolean flag categorical input, 0/1 categorical, rather numeric residual. erode dilate single class terms - rather symmetric, every-class--behaviour - convert class binary mask first (e.g. landcover == 2) run operator instead.
code: n <- 25 landcover <- rast(nrows = n, ncols = n, xmin = 0, xmax = n, ymin = 0, ymax = n, crs = "local") values(landcover) <- 1 landcover[, 19:25] <- 2       # a solid class-2 block on the right landcover[12, 18] <- 2        # a stray class-2 cell poking into class 1 landcover[12, 6] <- NA        # an interior gap, well away from the class boundary landcover <- as.factor(landcover) k <- se_box(1)  spike_xy <- xyFromCell(landcover, cellFromRowCol(landcover, 12, 18)) notch_xy <- xyFromCell(landcover, cellFromRowCol(landcover, 12, 6))  par(mfrow = c(1, 3)) plot(landcover, col = c("forestgreen", "steelblue"), axes = FALSE, main = "landcover (spike + gap)") points(rbind(spike_xy, notch_xy), cex = 4, col = "red", lwd = 3)  plot(tophat(landcover, k), col = c("white", "firebrick"), axes = FALSE, main = "tophat(landcover, se_box(1))") points(spike_xy, cex = 4, col = "black", lwd = 2)  plot(bottomhat(landcover, k), col = c("white", "firebrick"), axes = FALSE, main = "bottomhat(landcover, se_box(1))") points(notch_xy, cex = 4, col = "black", lwd = 2)

code: vec_ref <- shape_indices(subset_union, which = c("hull_ratio", "polsby_popper", "depth", "moment_of_inertia")) round(vec_ref, 4) moment_of_inertia             depth        hull_ratio     polsby_popper            0.8155            0.6853            0.8306            0.5140 rasterize_at <- function(vect_poly, n) {   bb <- ext(vect_poly)   cellsize <- max(bb$xmax - bb$xmin, bb$ymax - bb$ymin) / n   template <- rast(bb, resolution = cellsize, crs = crs(vect_poly))   rasterize(vect_poly, template, field = 1, background = 0) }  convergence <- do.call(rbind, lapply(c(25, 50, 100, 200), function(n) {   r <- rasterize_at(subset_vect, n)   n_valid <- as.numeric(global(r == 1, "sum", na.rm = TRUE))   res <- gm_shape_indices(r, which = c("hull_ratio", "polsby_popper", "depth", "moment_of_inertia"),                            size = 3000, seed = 1)   data.frame(n_cells_per_side = n, n_valid_cells = n_valid, t(res)) })) knitr::kable(convergence, digits = 4, row.names = FALSE) vec_weighted <- shape_indices_sf(subset, byrow = FALSE, weights = "BIR74", id = "triangle") vec_w <- st_drop_geometry(vec_weighted)[, c("moment_of_inertia_index", "convexity_index", "hull_ratio_index")]  r_bir74 <- rasterize(subset_vect, rasterize_at(subset_vect, 200), field = "BIR74") gm_w <- gm_shape_indices(r_bir74, which = c("moment_of_inertia", "convexity", "hull_ratio"),                           weighted = TRUE, size = 1500, seed = 1)  comparison <- rbind(vector = unlist(vec_w), raster = gm_w[c("moment_of_inertia", "convexity", "hull_ratio")]) knitr::kable(comparison, digits = 4)

Reconstructing function’s raw, pre-reference-fix formula given - using geometric quantities function already returns (area, perimeter, mbc_area, hull_perimeter) - shows four undershooting 1 well outside reasonable rounding: Comparing four functions actually return today shows fix effect - every one lands close 1:
code: make_disk <- function(n = 81, radius = 30) {   r <- rast(nrows = n, ncols = n, xmin = 0, xmax = n, ymin = 0, ymax = n, crs = "local")   cx <- init(r, "x") - n / 2; cy <- init(r, "y") - n / 2   ifel(sqrt(cx^2 + cy^2) <= radius, 1, 0) } disk <- make_disk()  depth   <- gm_depth_index(disk) pp      <- gm_polsby_popper_index(disk) reock   <- gm_reock_index(disk) detour  <- gm_detour_index(disk)  raw_scores <- c(   depth          = depth$mean_depth / (sqrt(depth$area / pi) / 3),   polsby_popper  = 4 * pi * pp$area / pp$perimeter^2,   reock          = reock$area / reock$mbc_area,   detour         = 2 * sqrt(pi * detour$area) / detour$hull_perimeter ) knitr::kable(round(raw_scores, 3), col.names = "raw score (a perfect disk should give 1)") fixed_scores <- c(depth = depth$index, polsby_popper = pp$index,                    reock = reock$index, detour = detour$index) knitr::kable(round(fixed_scores, 3), col.names = "gridmorph's actual index")
